extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won stage 10 in a technical sprint finale after Michael Morkov delivered a perfectly-timed 100m leadout on narrow roads.
tactical_patterns: Peloton suppressed breakaway to sub-2min from gun, indicating sprinter teams prioritized control due to scarcity of remaining sprint stages.
Teams prioritized positioning before km87 right turn onto narrow roads, creating pre-finale bottleneck stress point 80km from finish.
Morkov's 100m leadout launch point proved optimal against premature Greipel acceleration, suggesting shorter final windups succeed on technical finishes.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: First Irish stage win since Roche 1992, took green jersey with 21-point lead suggesting genuine Sagan threat
rider_signals.MORKOV Michael: positive: Perfectly calibrated 100m leadout timing superior to rival trains, critical domestique skill demonstration
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Finished third but lost green jersey by 21 points on pure sprint stage, signals declining sprint dominance
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: neutral: Showed return-to-form speed but launched too early, got chopped on corner positioning
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: First Slovenian yellow jersey holder navigated stress-filled flat stage without GC gaps, validated crash-avoidance skills
